task_completion.description: The agent opens the image in an image viewer or editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing the original landscape image (800x553 pixels) open in a GUI application like Image Viewer or GIMP.

task_completion.description: The agent locates the rotation controls in the application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing the agent opening a menu or hovering over a toolbar that contains rotation options.

task_completion.description: The agent selects the option to rotate the image 90 degrees counter-clockwise.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing the cursor clicking on 'Rotate Left', 'Rotate 90° Counter-Clockwise', or a corresponding icon (e.g., a circular arrow pointing left).

task_completion.description: The image is visibly rotated 90 degrees counter-clockwise.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing the image in the application canvas now in portrait orientation (taller than it is wide), confirming the rotation was applied.

task_completion.description: The agent saves the rotated image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing the agent clicking 'Save', 'Save As', pressing Ctrl+S, or confirming a save dialog before closing the application.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical interface to rotate the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a GUI application (e.g., Image Viewer, GIMP) rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent saved the rotated image to the file system.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent explicitly saves the file, rather than just rotating the view temporarily without saving.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a script or terminal command to perform the rotation.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing commands like `convert`, `mogrify`, or python scripts used for rotation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original image is a landscape Unsplash photo (800x553 pixels). After a 90-degree counter-clockwise rotation, the image should be in portrait orientation (553x800 pixels). The rotation must be counter-clockwise (often labeled as 'Rotate Left' in GUIs). The task requires saving the rotated image so the changes persist.
